hero.title: About Me
hero.subtitle: I’m a Product Developer and Designer who builds modern web applications with a strong focus on usability, performance, and clean architecture.
hero.description: With a background in both design and engineering, I bridge the gap between visual creativity and technical execution. My goal is simple: create digital products that are beautiful, intuitive, and scalable.
hero.works: I work with companies and startups around the world to transform ideas into high-quality web experiences — also helping women launch tech careers through Tech Beach Mama.

background.title: My Background
background.description: My path into tech started from the design side.
background.description2: I began my career working with graphic design and user experience, helping brands communicate visually and craft better digital products. Over time, I became increasingly interested in the technical side of building those experiences.
background.description3: That curiosity led me to transition into frontend development, where I could combine design thinking with engineering.
background.description4: Today, I specialize in building web applications with AI-assisted development with Cursor & Claude Code using modern technologies like React and Next.js, focusing on performance, scalability, and exceptional user experience.
background.description5: With experience working in professional development teams and delivering production-ready applications, I understand what it takes to build software that is not only visually polished but also maintainable and reliable.

whatIDo.title: What I Do
whatIDo.description: I help companies design and build modern digital products with AI.
whatIDo.workFocus: My work focuses on three areas:
whatIDo.fastBuilding: Building fast, scalable, and maintainable web applications using modern technologies.
whatIDo.intuitiveDesign: Designing interfaces that are intuitive, elegant, and user-focused.
whatIDo.designDrivenDevelopmentDescription: Combining design and development to create products where form and function work together seamlessly.

approach.title: My Approach
approach.description: I believe great digital products are created when design and engineering work together from the beginning.
approach.iFocusOn: Instead of simply implementing designs, I focus on solving real product problems and building experiences that users actually enjoy using.
approach.alsoPassionateAbout: I’m also passionate about building reusable UI systems and scalable frontend architectures.

quote.description: Ideas bloom where the mind wanders, not just where the screen glows.

contact.description: If you’re looking for a developer who understands both product design and frontend engineering, I’d love to hear about your project.

experience.title: My long experience counts on
experience.scalableWebApplications: years of Building scalable web applications

techBeachMama.title: Beyond client work
techBeachMama.description: I’m also building Tech Beach Mama — a platform where I help women, especially single mothers, transition into tech and build independent, flexible careers.
techBeachMama.description2: It’s where I share my journey, resources, and practical guidance for creating a life with more freedom.
techBeachMama.linkText: → Learn more about Tech Beach Mama

hero.title: My services
hero.subtitle: Website Development & Design. I help businesses build fast, scalable, and beautifully designed web experiences using the latest AI-assisted tools.
hero.description: I help you bring your ideas to life with a clean design and reliable frontend development — creating websites and applications that are both beautiful and built to perform.

cards.title: What I can help you with
cards.description: I combine frontend development, UX/UI design, AI and product thinking to create digital experiences that are both beautiful and built to perform.

process.title: My process
process.description: My process is simple and straightforward. I follow a step-by-step approach to build your project.

steps.discoveryDescription: Understanding your goals, audience, and vision to define the right direction.
steps.designDescription: Creating intuitive layouts and a visual identity that reflects your brand.
steps.developmentDescription: Building a fast, scalable, and reliable product using modern technologies.
steps.launchDescription: Testing, refining, and launching your project with performance in mind.

quote.description: I combine engineering precision with design intuition — so your product not only works, but feels right.

testimonials.title: What my clients say about me
testimonials.subtitle: I have worked with a variety of clients, from startups to large corporations. Here is what they have to say about me.

list.name: Fernando Rueda Oliva
list.position: CTO Quantum engineer and Solutions Architect
list.testimonial: I’ve had the pleasure of working alongside Asa at Hexad for several years, and I can confidently say she is one of the most adaptable, positive, and inspiring colleagues I’ve ever met. Asa’s attitude is truly exceptional—no matter how complex or unfamiliar the challenge, she always greets it with a smile and an open mind. Her journey from a talented UI designer to a skilled product engineer is a testament to her courage and determination to grow. Not only did she master new technical skills, but she also stepped up to take on the responsibilities of a Project Manager, seamlessly adapting to each new role with enthusiasm and professionalism. What sets Asa apart is her flexibility and her fearless approach to change. She never shies away from stepping outside her comfort zone, and her positive energy uplifts everyone around her. Asa’s ability to wear multiple hats, learn quickly, and maintain a can-do attitude has been invaluable to our team. Thank you, Asa, for your dedication, your positivity, and for all the years you’ve contributed to Hexad. It’s been a privilege to work with you, and I look forward to seeing all the great things you’ll accomplish in the future!

list.name: Catalina Golia
list.testimonial: It was a pleasure working with her on the development of my website. She understood exactly what I needed from the very beginning, worked with great speed and dedication, and provided very valuable advice that significantly improved the final result. I would highlight both her strong technical expertise and her close, professional approach, which made the entire process smooth and efficient. I highly recommend her as an expert web developer. Thank you Asa!

cta.title: Work with me

Whether you need a complete website, a redesign, or support improving an existing platform, I bring a structured approach from concept to launch.

techBeachMama.title: Tech Beach Mama
techBeachMama.description: A personal brand platform and AI product business — designed and built in Next.js with an AI-powered lead magnet that generates personalized product roadmaps. Repositioned in 2026 from career education to helping women with tech skills ship their first paying AI product.

hero.subtitle: Building a personal brand, an AI-powered funnel, and a digital product business from scratch
hero.description: Tech Beach Mama is not just a website — it is a growing platform, a real business, and a live experiment in building AI products in public. Originally launched to help women start frontend careers, it was strategically repositioned in 2026 when the market shifted: the new mission is helping women who already have tech skills go from idea to their first paying AI product. The centrepiece is the AI Product Blueprint — an interactive AI tool I designed, built, and shipped end to end.

step1.label: The Vision
step1.title: A platform that repositioned itself when the market did
textItems: Tech Beach Mama started after I left employment to go fully independent, with a mission to help women — particularly mothers — build tech careers the way I had.

In 2026 I made a strategic decision most brands avoid: I repositioned the entire platform. Watching AI absorb exactly the junior roles beginners were training for, I stopped selling a promise the market was breaking — and rebuilt TBM around a new one: women who already have tech skills can now build and sell what used to take a team. The gap isn't skill; it's a plan.

That repositioning was executed as a full product cycle: new messaging, complete site rewrite, archived legacy products with 301 redirects, a rebuilt email funnel, and a brand-new AI product as the front door — all shipped in a matter of days using an AI-assisted development workflow.

challenges.title: Repositioning a live brand comes with its own set of constraints.
rows: Designed, built, and maintained entirely solo alongside freelance client work
Repositioning had to preserve SEO, past customers, and brand equity
The new lead magnet is a real AI product — with real API costs, abuse protection, and GDPR obligations
Every claim on the site had to be honest — the brand's core promise is 'building in public, real numbers included'

step2.label: Design Concept
step2.title: Clean, warm, and built to convert
textItems: The design was created in Figma with conversion as a first-class concern alongside aesthetics. The visual language needed to feel warm, aspirational, and credible — communicating that this is a serious resource built by someone who actually ships what she teaches. After the repositioning, every section funnels toward a single action: getting your free AI Product Blueprint. One front door, one promise, no competing calls to action.
step2.alt: Tech Beach Mama design concept — Figma
step2.caption: Design concept — Figma

step3.label: Stack & Architecture
step3.title: Built for performance, flexibility, and a real AI product
textItems: The stack was chosen for what this platform needs to do over the long term: fast performance, easy iteration, and the flexibility to ship real products — not just content.

Next.js handles server-side rendering and routing, Tailwind CSS and Shadcn UI keep the design system composable, and the Blueprint added a full product backend: the OpenAI API for generation, Neon Postgres for submissions and rate limiting, MailerLite for audience segmentation, and Nodemailer for transactional email delivery. The repository is private — this is a live commercial project.

solution.title: The technical stack now supports a live AI product, not just a content site.
rows: Next.js for performance, SEO, and scalable routing
OpenAI API (server-side only) generating personalized product roadmaps
Neon Postgres storing submissions — doubling as market-research data
Rate limiting per email and IP protecting a public AI endpoint
MailerLite segmentation: newsletter and Blueprint-user audiences
Nodemailer delivering each user's roadmap by email

step4.label: The Funnel
step4.title: From social to an AI-generated personal plan to email list
textItems: The platform is built around a deliberate conversion path. Visitors arrive from Instagram and LinkedIn, where the brand shares the build-in-public journey. The website captures that interest with the AI Product Blueprint — a free, interactive tool that turns a visitor's idea, skills, and available hours into a personalized step-by-step plan to her first paying AI product.

The Blueprint is a smarter lead magnet than any PDF: it demonstrates the exact skill the brand teaches, it captures qualified emails, and every questionnaire submission is simultaneously market research — the answers reveal exactly which products to build next. The funnel doesn't just convert; it learns.
step4.alt: Tech Beach Mama live site — the AI Product Blueprint funnel
step4.caption: The live funnel — one front door: the AI Product Blueprint

step5.label: The AI Product
step5.title: The Blueprint — a shipped AI product, not a demo
textItems: The AI Product Blueprint is a complete production AI application: a nine-question interactive flow feeds a carefully engineered generation prompt that produces a personalized roadmap — her idea sharpened, her unfair advantages, a four-phase path sized to her real weekly hours, her biggest blocker addressed, and one concrete action for this week.

The engineering behind it reflects real product discipline: the prompt was voice-tuned through systematic side-by-side model comparisons, guarded against prompt injection and off-topic input, and hard-ruled to never promise income or invent specifics the user didn't provide. Results render on screen and arrive by email; every submission is stored with hashed IPs for GDPR-conscious rate limiting.

offerings.title: What shipping the Blueprint involved:
rows: Nine-question interactive questionnaire with per-type validation
Voice-tuned generation prompt, tested across models with real personas
Prompt-injection detection returning safe, typed errors
Rate limiting (per email and IP) and API spending caps
Dual delivery: styled on-screen results plus transactional email
Privacy policy and consent flow updated for AI data processing

step5.alt: The AI Product Blueprint — interactive questionnaire and generated roadmap
step5.caption: The Blueprint — from nine answers to a personalized product roadmap

step6.label: Performance & SEO
step6.title: Built to be found and fast to load
textItems: Performance and SEO were built in from the start, not added afterwards. Next.js server-side rendering ensures pages are indexable and fast, Tailwind keeps the CSS footprint minimal, and the component architecture avoids the bloat that slows content-heavy sites over time. The repositioning preserved SEO equity with 301 redirects for every archived route, a corrected sitemap, and an llms.txt file making the site legible to AI assistants.
For a platform that depends on organic discovery and social traffic converting into an email list, load speed and Core Web Vitals directly affect conversion rates — not just search rankings.
step6.alt: Tech Beach Mama Lighthouse performance scores
step6.caption: Performance · Lighthouse scores

step7.label: The Outcome
step7.title: A live AI product, a growing audience, and a business that learns
textItems: Tech Beach Mama is the project I am most personally invested in. It is simultaneously a demonstration of what I can build technically — including a complete AI product shipped solo — a real business with a live funnel, and the public proof behind my positioning as an AI Product Engineer.
It is also a live experiment in honest marketing: the brand's promise is building in public with real numbers, and the platform itself is case study number one.

results.title: What the project demonstrates:
rows: End-to-end AI product delivery — design, prompts, backend, delivery, compliance
Strategic repositioning of a live brand, executed as a full product cycle
Conversion-focused design applied to a real, learning funnel
Production-grade Next.js + OpenAI + Postgres architecture built solo
A platform that continues to grow — this case study will keep evolving

finalCta.title: Want an AI product built from idea to launch?

sculptedGoddess.title: Sculpted Goddess
sculptedGoddess.description: A nutrition and fitness web application — originally built as a CS50 final project in Python and Flask, later rebuilt from the ground up in Next.js and TypeScript to demonstrate modern frontend architecture, performance, and long-term maintainability.

hero.subtitle: From a CS50 final project to a modern, full-stack fitness platform
hero.description: Sculpted Goddess started as a data-driven nutrition and fitness web application, built as the final project for Harvard's CS50x Introduction to Computer Science course. Years later, it became a vehicle for something more: a full architectural rebuild in Next.js and TypeScript, demonstrating how legacy applications can be modernised without losing their core purpose. The project tracks personal fitness data, calculates macronutrient needs, and lets users search and save recipes — all rebuilt on a foundation designed to last.

step1.label: The Origin
step1.title: A CS50 final project built in Python and Flask
textItems: Sculpted Goddess was created as the final project for Harvard's CS50x Introduction to Computer Science course — a nutrition and fitness tool built to help women set up personalised meal plans and work toward their fitness goals.

The original version was built with Python, Flask, SQL, JavaScript, HTML, and SCSS. It connected to the Edamam Recipe Search API and handled user authentication, a macronutrient calculator, and recipe search and saving — all server-side rendered. The design was created in Adobe XD before a single line of code was written.

The application worked, but over time it became increasingly difficult to maintain and extend. The Python knowledge faded, the API integration aged, and the architecture wasn't built to scale. That gap between the original idea and what the technology could actually support became the motivation for a complete rebuild.

challenges.title: The original setup had served its purpose, but came with real long-term limitations.
rows: Python/Flask stack no longer actively maintained
Edamam API integration needed replacing with MealDB
Architecture not designed to scale or extend
Original Adobe XD design file lost — rebuild required recreating from scratch

step2.label: Design Concept
step2.title: Starting with design — then and now
textItems: The original Sculpted Goddess was designed in Adobe XD before any code was written — a deliberate, design-first approach that carried into the rebuilt version. The visual language needed to feel strong and intentional: a platform for women who are serious about nutrition and fitness, not a generic wellness app. Clean structure, clear hierarchy, and a confident aesthetic that communicates trust and precision.
step2.alt: Sculpted Goddess original Adobe XD design concept
step2.caption: Original design concept — Adobe XD

step3.label: Rebuild & Architecture
step3.title: Rebuilding the platform on a modern stack
textItems: The rebuild was not a cosmetic update — it was a complete re-engineering of the application from the ground up. The goal was to keep the core product logic intact while replacing the technical foundation with something modern, maintainable, and scalable.

The new stack uses Next.js with TypeScript for server-side rendering and type safety, SCSS for styling, and libSQL for the database layer. The Edamam API integration was replaced with MealDB, a free and actively maintained recipe API. User authentication, recipe saving, and the macronutrient calculator were all rebuilt with the same feature scope — but with significantly cleaner architecture underneath.

solution.title: The rebuild focused on architectural quality and long-term maintainability.
rows: Migrated from Python/Flask to Next.js with TypeScript
Replaced deprecated Edamam API with MealDB integration
Rebuilt database layer using libSQL
Preserved all core features: recipe search, saving, macro calculator, contact form
Added full TypeScript coverage for type safety and developer confidence
Redesigned the user experience for clarity and mobile responsiveness

step4.label: Core Features
step4.title: What the platform does
textItems: Sculpted Goddess is built around four main features: a recipe search powered by the MealDB API with individual nutritional data per recipe, a personal recipe library where logged-in users can save and revisit their favourites, a macronutrient and BMI calculator that takes personal data and fitness goals as input, and a contact form for direct client enquiries.

The platform is open to browse for anyone, but signing up and logging in unlocks the full feature set — recipe saving, personal data, and macro tracking. This structure keeps the barrier to entry low while creating a meaningful reason to register.
step4.alt: Sculpted Goddess live — recipe search and nutrition features
step4.caption: The rebuilt platform live — recipe search, nutrition data, and personal macro tracking

step5.label: Technical Evolution
step5.title: From Flask to Next.js — what changed and why
textItems: The technical improvements in the rebuild are not simply about switching frameworks. They reflect a fundamentally different approach to how the application is structured, typed, and maintained over time.

Note: Python and Flask are excellent tools for many use cases. The decision to rebuild in Next.js was driven by the specific goals of this project: demonstrating modern frontend architecture, improving long-term maintainability, and aligning the codebase with the stack I use for client work.

before.title: Original (Python / Flask)
rows: Server-side rendering with Flask
SQL database, no type safety
Edamam Recipe API (now replaced)
Plain JavaScript, no TypeScript
Limited architecture scalability

after.title: Rebuilt (Next.js / TypeScript)
rows: Next.js with server-side rendering
libSQL database with TypeScript throughout
MealDB API — free, maintained, flexible
Full TypeScript coverage
Clean, scalable frontend architecture

step5.alt: Lighthouse performance scores — Python/Flask before vs Next.js after
step5.caption: Performance · Python/Flask vs Next.js (Lighthouse)

step6.label: Design System
step6.title: A structured SCSS design system built for consistency across the application
textItems: Sculpted Goddess is styled through a structured SCSS architecture: colors.scss, fontSizes.scss, and breakPoints.scss are imported into a central constants.module.scss that exports all design tokens directly to JavaScript components. Spacing, typography, breakpoints, and colour values are defined once and consumed throughout the application — no magic numbers, no repeated hex codes.

The typography system uses two distinct typefaces with clear roles: Cinzel (serif) for h1 headings — uppercase, high-impact, and fitting for the brand — and Open Sans for all body text, labels, and navigation. Font sizes are defined across three explicit breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop) via CSS custom properties set in globals.scss, giving every text element a consistent, predictable scale across devices.
step6.alt: Sculpted Goddess design system — colour palette and responsive typography scale
step6.caption: Design system · SCSS colour tokens and responsive type scale

step7.label: The Outcome
step7.title: A personal project that became a professional case study
textItems: Sculpted Goddess grew from a course assignment into a real demonstration of how I approach complex problems: starting with design, building with intention, and being willing to rebuild something entirely when the foundation no longer serves the product.

This project reflects exactly the kind of work I do for clients — modernising legacy applications, migrating to scalable architectures, and rebuilding products without losing the core business logic that made them valuable in the first place.

results.title: The rebuild resulted in:
rows: A fully modernised stack built on Next.js and TypeScript
Improved performance and Core Web Vitals
A clean, maintainable codebase built to extend
Active, maintained API integration via MealDB
A demonstrable example of modern frontend architecture for client conversations

finalCta.title: Working with a legacy codebase that needs modernising?

cgConsulting.title: CG Consulting
cgConsulting.description: A complete redesign and platform migration for a Spanish HR consultancy — from a fragile WordPress setup to a fast, bilingual Next.js web app with Sanity CMS, built to last without ongoing maintenance costs.

hero.subtitle: From WordPress to a scalable, high-performance web architecture
hero.description: A complete redesign and rebuild of a consulting website, focused on performance, maintainability, and a clean, professional user experience. Many small businesses rely on WordPress setups that become fragile over time. This project shows how migrating to a modern stack can reduce long-term risk while improving performance and flexibility.

step1.label: The Problem
step1.title: A WordPress site held together by fragile plugins
textItems: CG Consulting is a boutique HR services firm helping international companies find talent in Spain.

The existing WordPress website had served the business for years but had become increasingly fragile due to plugin dependencies and lack of ongoing maintenance. They needed a stable, professional digital presence that could be managed without relying on a developer for day-to-day updates — in both Spanish and English.
The goal of this project was not only to redesign the website, but to rebuild it on a modern, scalable architecture that removes technical risk and supports future growth.

challenges.title: The main challenge was not the platform itself, but the long-term sustainability of the existing setup.
rows: Plugin conflicts risking content loss
No budget for ongoing maintenance
Slow page loads, no performance control
Client dependent on developer for updates

step2.label: Design Concept
step2.title: Starting with design, not code
textItems: The redesign centered on clarity and credibility — the two things an independent HR consultant needs to communicate immediately. I focused on a clean visual hierarchy, structured content presentation, and a layout that feels professional without being cold. Subtle animations and transitions, built with Framer Motion, add movement and polish to the experience without pulling attention away from the content. The site is fully responsive, designed to feel equally trustworthy on mobile and desktop.
step2.caption: Redesign concept — Figma

step3.label: Migration & Architecture
step3.title: Moving from WordPress to a modern stack
textItems: I proposed a full platform migration from WordPress to a modern Next.js + Sanity CMS stack. The goal wasn't just a technical upgrade — it was to give the client genuine ownership of their content and confidence that their site wouldn't break without a developer on call.
Since CG Consulting works with international companies to source talent in Spain, the site needed to speak to two audiences. Full bilingual support in Spanish and English was built in from the start.

solution.title: I rebuilt the website using a modern, lightweight architecture focused on simplicity and control.
rows: Removed dependency on plugins
Introduced structured content via Sanity CMS
Built a custom frontend for full control over performance and design
Implemented a responsive, mobile-first layout
Added subtle animations to enhance the user experience

step4.label: The Live Site
step4.title: Design in the browser — desktop & mobile
textItems: The redesigned site live — clean layout, professional credibility, and a fully responsive experience across all devices.
step4.caption: The redesigned site live — clean layout, professional credibility, and a fully responsive experience across all devices.

step5.label: Performance
step5.title: From slow to fast — measurable results
textItems: Performance and SEO improvements are not tied to a specific framework, but to how the application is built. In this project, the improvements came from simplifying the architecture, reducing dependencies, and optimizing the implementation.

Note: WordPress can absolutely deliver excellent performance and SEO when built with a clean architecture and minimal dependencies. In this case, the chosen approach prioritized long-term stability, performance control, and a simplified maintenance experience for the client.

before.title: Before
rows: Plugin-heavy WordPress setup
Recurring maintenance issues
Slower performance
Limited flexibility

after.title: After
rows: Lightweight custom architecture
Stable and predictable setup
Optimized performance
Full control over frontend and content

step5.alt: Lighthouse performance scores — WordPress before vs Next.js after
step5.caption: Performance · WordPress vs Next.js (Lighthouse)

step6.label: Client Handoff
step6.title: CG Consulting can manage their own content — no developer needed
textItems: Sanity CMS was configured to give the client a clean, simple editing interface. They can update their services, copy, and contact info without touching code — no developer required after launch.
step6.alt: Sanity Studio content management interface for CG Consulting
step6.caption: Content management · Sanity Studio

step7.label: The Outcome
step7.title: A stable, bilingual web app built to last
textItems: The client went from worrying about plugin conflicts to having full control over her content through a clean CMS interface. The new site is significantly faster, far more stable, and built on a foundation that can scale as her business does — without depending on a developer for day-to-day updates.
This project reflects how I approach client work: solving a real business problem, not just building a website.

results.title: The new setup resulted in:
rows: Faster load times
Improved Core Web Vitals
Cleaner, more maintainable codebase
Easier content management
Reduced long-term maintenance needs

finalCta.title: Looking to modernize your website or improve performance?

matchKit.title: Match Kit
matchKit.description: An AI-powered CV analyser that matches your resume against any job description — returning a match score, skill gaps, missing keywords and rewrite suggestions. Built with Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS and the Gemini AI API.

hero.subtitle: From frustration to product — an AI tool built to solve a real problem
hero.description: MatchKit started as a personal frustration. After sending out countless job applications and hearing nothing back, I decided to build a tool that tells you exactly how well your CV matches a job description — and what to fix. This project demonstrates how modern AI APIs can be integrated into a production-ready Next.js application with a clean, considered user experience.

step1.label: The Problem
step1.title: Sending CVs into the void
textItems: In 2026, the frontend job market is saturated. Recruiters receive hundreds of applications per role, and most CVs are filtered by ATS systems before a human ever reads them.
The core problem is that most candidates have no idea how well their CV actually matches a specific job description. They apply broadly, hear nothing back, and have no signal for what to improve.

I wanted to build something that gives immediate, actionable feedback — a match score, identified gaps, missing keywords, and concrete rewrite suggestions — so candidates can stop guessing and start improving.

challenges.title: The problem had several layers that a simple keyword checker couldn't solve.
rows: CVs are not optimised per role — candidates use one version for everything
ATS systems filter on keywords before humans are involved
Generic feedback tools give vague advice, not specific rewrites
Candidates have no benchmark for what 'good enough' looks like

step2.label: Design Concept
step2.title: Designing for clarity under pressure
textItems: The design needed to feel sharp, trustworthy and fast — the opposite of a generic SaaS dashboard. I chose a dark-mode aesthetic with a single high-contrast accent colour, tight typographic hierarchy using DM Sans and DM Mono, and a deliberately minimal layout that puts the analysis results at the centre of attention.

Every design decision was driven by the emotional state of the user: someone anxious about their job search who needs clear, honest information — not noise. The result is a UI that feels clinical in the best sense — precise, readable and calm.
step2.caption: Design concept — dark mode, DM Sans + DM Mono type pairing

step3.label: Architecture
step3.title: AI integration with a secure, server-side API proxy
textItems: The core technical challenge was integrating a third-party AI API cleanly and securely. Rather than exposing an API key client-side — a common mistake in AI demos — I built a server-side route handler in Next.js that proxies all requests to the Gemini API. The key never leaves the server.

The AI response is structured via a carefully engineered system prompt that forces the model to return strict JSON every time — match score, summary, strengths, gaps, keywords and before/after rewrite pairs. This makes the response reliably parseable and directly renderable in the UI without post-processing guesswork.
The result is a clean separation between the AI layer and the presentation layer — a pattern that scales to any AI-powered product.

solution.title: Key technical decisions that make this production-ready, not just a demo.
rows: Server-side API proxy via Next.js route handler — API key never exposed to client
Strict JSON output enforced through prompt engineering
Gemini 2.5 Flash for speed and cost efficiency on the free tier
TypeScript throughout — fully typed API response and component props
Tailwind CSS for a consistent, maintainable design system

step4.label: The Live App
step4.title: The analyser in action — desktop & mobile
textItems: The finished application — from the landing page through to a full analysis result with score, gaps, keywords and rewrite suggestions. Fully responsive across all screen sizes.
step4.alt: MatchKit live application — analysis results screen
step4.caption: The live app — match score, gap analysis, keyword chips and rewrite suggestions.

step5.label: Prompt Engineering
step5.title: The AI is only as good as the instructions you give it
textItems: One of the most underestimated parts of building AI-powered products is prompt design. A poorly structured prompt returns inconsistent, unparseable output. A well-engineered one behaves like a reliable API.

For MatchKit, the system prompt instructs the model to act as a specialist CV analyst for frontend and tech roles, return only valid JSON with no preamble or markdown, produce rewrites that quote actual phrases from the CV, and ensure that strengths and gaps are specific to the job description — not generic advice.
This level of prompt precision is what separates a working prototype from a production-ready tool.
step5.alt: MatchKit system prompt and JSON output structure
step5.caption: Prompt engineering · Structured JSON output enforced via system instruction

step6.label: The Outcome
step6.title: A production-ready AI product built in a single sprint
textItems: MatchKit went from idea to deployed application in under a day. It demonstrates that AI integration does not require a complex backend or a large team — it requires clear product thinking, clean architecture, and disciplined prompt engineering.
This project reflects how I approach product work: identify a real problem, design a focused solution, and ship something that actually works.

results.title: What this project demonstrates to clients and employers:
rows: Ability to integrate third-party AI APIs securely and cleanly
Full-stack Next.js — client UI, server route handlers, environment config
Prompt engineering as a product skill, not just a technical one
Design sensibility applied to a functional tool, not just a marketing site
Fast, independent execution from concept to deployed product

step6.alt: MatchKit production deployment on Vercel — serverless function and environment configuration
step6.caption: Production deployment · Vercel serverless function with secure environment configuration

finalCta.title: Looking for a developer who can build AI-powered products from scratch?

hexadCarClientPlatform.title: Automotive Platform
hexadCarClientPlatform.description: Three years in one of the world's most demanding enterprise environments — as a UX/UI designer and frontend developer on a global vehicle connectivity platform used in over 40 countries and adapted for multiple vehicle brands within the same group.

hero.subtitle: Global vehicle platform — UX/UI design and frontend development at enterprise scale
hero.description: A multi-year engagement with one of the world's leading automotive groups: a digital onboarding platform connecting new vehicle owners with their car, their identity, and their services — across more than 40 countries, in multiple languages, adapted for different brands and local legal frameworks. I contributed in two roles: as a part-time UX/UI designer embedded in a cross-functional team, and later as a full-time developer within an agile product team.

step1.label: The Project
step1.title: An enterprise platform at global scale
textItems: The engagement concerned a mission-critical digital product within one of the world's largest automotive groups — an onboarding platform where new vehicle owners connect their car, accept legal terms, and activate digital services.

The complexity was exceptional: the same product had to work correctly in over 40 countries with different regulations, support multiple brands with their own design systems, and meet strict requirements from the group's design and legal departments.
The platform had zero tolerance for failure — a broken onboarding flow meant a customer could not access their vehicle's connected services from day one.

challenges.title: The core challenges spanned design, technology, and organisation.
rows: Legal variations across 40+ markets
Strict approval process via design and legal departments
Multiple brands with distinct design systems in a shared codebase
High reliability requirements in critical user flows
Coordination across a large, distributed multi-team environment

step2.label: Design Role
step2.title: UX/UI design within a complex approval structure
textItems: As a part-time UX/UI designer, my role was as much about communication and negotiation as visual design. Every design decision passed through a structured approval chain involving design, legal, content, and product teams — a process requiring precision, patience, and the ability to defend design decisions in front of multiple parallel stakeholders.

I collaborated daily with product owners, project managers, writers, developers, and QA — acting as a bridge between business requirements and user experience. Navigating that structure without losing design integrity was as important a skill as the craft itself.
step2.alt: Design process — cross-functional collaboration in an enterprise environment
step2.caption: Design process — collaboration, approvals, and iterations

step3.label: Global Adaptation
step3.title: One experience — across 40+ markets and multiple brands
textItems: One of the most complex design challenges was breadth of scale: the product had to absorb significant variation — legal frameworks, languages, local rules, and brand identities — without fragmenting.

The work involved adapting layouts, content structures, and legal flows within an existing enterprise design system and component library, with the goal of creating an architecture consistent enough to scale and flexible enough to absorb market variations.

solution.title: The design work focused on structural flexibility within fixed constraints.
rows: Adapting flows for local legal requirements
Consistent UX patterns despite high content variability
Working within an established enterprise design system
Coordinating approvals from design and legal departments
Supporting multiple brands with distinct visual identities

step4.label: Development Role
step4.title: Frontend architecture for a multi-brand product
textItems: As a full-time developer in React and TypeScript, I participated in architectural decisions from the start. The core technical challenge mirrored the design challenge: one codebase, multiple brand identities, more than 40 market variants.

The solution was built on a robust theming layer mapping brand-specific design tokens to a shared component library — the same component rendered correctly in each brand's visual language without duplicating business logic.

I contributed to architectural decisions alongside product owners, designers, QA, and stakeholders — a cross-functional collaboration requiring both technical depth and the ability to communicate tradeoffs clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
step4.alt: Frontend architecture — multi-brand theming with React and TypeScript
step4.caption: Architecture · Shared codebase with brand-specific design tokens

step5.label: Technical Complexity
step5.title: High reliability in critical legal flows
textItems: The onboarding flow placed high demands on correctness: users needed to navigate a series of legal declarations — privacy policies, consent forms, service agreements — varying by country and regulatory environment.

The frontend logic handled conditional rendering of the correct declarations in the correct order, under the correct conditions, while maintaining a brand-appropriate experience. Errors in this flow were not an option — the platform was mission-critical from day one for the user.

before.title: The Challenges
rows: Legal variation across 40+ markets
Multiple brands in a shared codebase
Complex conditional logic in critical flows
Strict approval process for every design change
Distributed multi-team environment with high quality standards

after.title: The Solutions
rows: Flexible theming layer with per-brand design tokens
Scalable component architecture for market variants
Reliable flows with high test coverage
Effective communication through the approval chain
Long-term code quality in a large, multi-team environment

step5.caption: Technical solution · Multi-brand theming and legal flows

step6.label: Stack and Collaboration
step6.title: React, TypeScript, and cross-functional teamwork at enterprise scale
textItems: The team consisted of full-stack developers in React and TypeScript, working closely with designers, product owners, QA, and the group's internal departments. Code quality, maintainability, and long-term stability were central requirements in an environment where the product was expected to live and be maintained for many years.
Working in a large, distributed multi-team environment with strict enterprise requirements meant that communication and documentation were as important as technical ability.
step6.alt: Agile team collaboration in an enterprise environment
step6.caption: Collaboration · Agile team with React, TypeScript, and cross-functional stakeholders

step7.label: The Outcome
step7.title: Mission-critical platform in production — globally, across multiple brands
textItems: A digital onboarding platform in production for one of the world's leading automotive groups — used by vehicle buyers in over 40 countries, adapted for multiple brands within the group, and built to last in a complex multi-team environment.
This engagement represents the kind of enterprise environment where detail, communication, and technical precision are not optional — and that is the experience I bring to every project.

results.title: What this engagement demonstrates:
rows: Ability to deliver within a strict enterprise approval process
Experience with global product scale and legal complexity
Multi-brand frontend architecture with React and TypeScript
Cross-functional collaboration at all levels — design, legal, product, QA
Long-term code quality and maintainability in distributed teams

finalCta.title: Do you need a developer with experience in complex enterprise environments?

casaMadre.title: Casa Madre
casaMadre.description: A bilingual boutique real-estate platform for a Barcelona agency — designed and built in Next.js with Sanity CMS, an AI writing assistant that drafts elegant property listings from structured facts, and a fully self-service editorial workflow for the client.

hero.subtitle: A boutique real-estate brand online — with AI woven into the client's daily workflow
hero.description: Casa Madre is a boutique real-estate agency in Barcelona run by two founding agents. They needed more than a brochure website: a bilingual platform that presents properties with editorial elegance, a content system they can manage entirely themselves, and — the differentiator — an AI writing assistant inside their CMS that drafts polished Spanish and English property listings from the facts they enter. Designed, built, and delivered end to end as a solo engagement.

step1.label: The Client
step1.title: A boutique agency that needed independence, not just a website
textItems: Casa Madre helps clients buy, sell, and rent distinctive homes in Barcelona's most characterful neighbourhoods. As a two-person boutique, their brand depends on a personal, editorial feel — the opposite of the portal-listing aesthetic that dominates real estate.

The brief had a clear underlying requirement: the founders needed to run their digital presence themselves — publishing properties, writing journal articles, updating their own team cards — without a developer on call for every change. In both Spanish and English, for an international clientele.
The answer was a structured content platform with AI assistance built into the editorial workflow itself: the agents enter the facts, and the system helps write the words.

challenges.title: A boutique brand with enterprise-grade content requirements.
rows: Fully bilingual content (ES/EN) across properties, journal, and legal pages
Client self-service for every content type — zero developer dependency
AI-generated listing copy that must never invent facts about a property
GDPR and Spanish LSSI compliance: consent-aware analytics, privacy-friendly maps, complete legal pages

step2.label: Design System
step2.title: Quiet luxury — an editorial design language for real estate
textItems: The design language was built around restraint: a warm palette of cream, clay, and deep brown, serif display typography, generous white space, and subtle motion that respects reduced-motion preferences. A cinematic video hero and Ken Burns imagery give the property presentation an editorial, magazine-like quality — deliberately unlike the crowded aesthetic of property portals. Every component follows the same tokens, spacing, and motion vocabulary, so the site feels like one considered object rather than assembled sections.
step2.alt: Casa Madre design system — palette, typography, and components
step2.caption: Design system — quiet luxury tokens, serif typography, editorial motion

step3.label: Stack & Architecture
step3.title: Next.js and Sanity — structured content the client owns
textItems: The platform is built on Next.js with TypeScript and Sanity CMS as the structured content backbone. Properties, neighbourhoods, journal articles, and the agent team all live as typed Sanity documents, edited in a Studio customised in Spanish for the client, with English slugs for international SEO.

Bilingual content runs through internationalized fields with locale-aware queries, next-intl handles the frontend routing, and the contact funnel delivers enquiries to both agents simultaneously via a validated, rate-limited, honeypot-protected API route with SMTP delivery. Privacy decisions were architectural: a static, consent-free map card instead of an embedded tracker, and analytics gated behind cookie consent.

solution.title: An architecture designed for client independence and compliance.
rows: Sanity CMS with typed schemas: properties, neighbourhoods, journal, team
Full ES/EN internationalization with SEO-friendly English slugs and 301s
Contact API with server-side validation, rate limiting, and honeypot protection
Enquiries delivered to both agents with reply-to routing
GDPR-first choices: consent-gated analytics, privacy-friendly static map
Complete Spanish legal framework: LSSI legal notice, privacy, cookies

step4.label: Bilingual Content
step4.title: One Studio, two languages, zero developer dependency
textItems: Every content type on the platform — property listings with galleries and filters, neighbourhood guides, journal articles, the founders' own contact cards with photos — is fully editable by the client in Sanity Studio, in both languages. The Studio is configured in Spanish with helper text guiding correct data entry, and the client began managing their own content (including uploading their team photos) before the project was even handed over — the clearest possible validation of a self-service architecture.
step4.alt: Casa Madre in Sanity Studio — bilingual property editing
step4.caption: Sanity Studio — bilingual editing, configured for the client in Spanish

step5.label: AI Writing Assistant
step5.title: AI that drafts elegant listings — and never invents a square metre
textItems: The platform's differentiator is an AI writing assistant built directly into the client's editorial workflow. For journal articles, it drafts editorial content from a topic. For property listings, it goes further: the agent fills in the structured facts — operation, type, neighbourhood, price, size, rooms — adds optional notes, and the assistant generates an evocative title, a two-to-three paragraph description written natively in both Spanish and English, and suggested highlight tags.

The prompt engineering is where the real product thinking lives: the assistant writes in the brand's editorial voice with a hard ban on portal clichés — and a stricter rule for real estate: it may only use facts the agent provided. No invented terraces, no imagined renovations. The agent reviews everything in a preview and inserts per field; a human approves every word before it publishes. The API key stays server-side, on the client's own AI account.
step5.alt: Casa Madre AI writing assistant — generating a bilingual property listing in Sanity Studio
step5.caption: The AI composer — structured facts in, bilingual editorial copy out, human approval always

step6.label: Performance
step6.title: Editorial imagery without the editorial load times
textItems: An image-heavy real-estate site lives or dies on performance. Sanity's image pipeline delivers hotspot-cropped, responsive assets; Next.js server rendering keeps every page indexable; and the deliberate absence of third-party embeds — no map SDK, no pre-consent scripts — keeps the loading waterfall clean. The result is a visually rich site that still scores where it matters for both SEO and the impatient property browser.
step6.caption: Performance · Lighthouse scores

step7.label: The Outcome
step7.title: A live platform the client runs herself — with AI as a daily tool
textItems: Casa Madre launched on its own domain with the full platform live: bilingual property listings, journal, personal agent cards, a working enquiry funnel to both founders, and an AI assistant the agents use as part of their normal publishing workflow — not as a gimmick, but as the tool that turns their property facts into publication-ready copy in two languages.

This project reflects the kind of work I focus on as an AI Product Engineer: not adding AI for its own sake, but embedding it precisely where it saves a real client real hours — inside the workflow they already use, with guardrails that protect their brand and their compliance.

results.title: What the engagement delivered:
rows: A complete bilingual platform the two-person client team manages alone
An AI listing composer producing native ES/EN editorial copy from structured facts
Strict AI guardrails: brand voice enforced, invented details impossible, human approval always
A compliant foundation: LSSI legal pages, consent-gated analytics, privacy-first choices
A working enquiry funnel delivering to both agents with reply-to routing

finalCta.title: Want AI built into your business where it actually saves time?
